xEnrich 0.1.0
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Changelog

Run GSEA across a collection of gene sets

Source: R/run_batch_gsea.R
run_batch_gsea.Rd

Applies run_enrichment(..., method = "gsea") to every pathway in gene_sets and returns both a named list of per-pathway results (compatible with run_gsea_redundancy_selection) and a summary data frame with BH-adjusted p-values across all pathways.

run_batch_gsea(
  gene_sets,
  gene_stats,
  n_perm = 1000L,
  adaptive = TRUE,
  adaptive_mode = c("refine", "pvalue", "decision"),
  alpha = 0.05,
  eps = 0.01,
  min_size = 10L,
  max_size = 500L,
  nthreads = 1L,
  seed = NULL,
  ...
)

Arguments

gene_sets

Named list of character vectors (pathways to test).

gene_stats

Named numeric vector of per-gene scores (e.g. log2FC, t-statistic, signed -log10 p). Names are gene symbols and define the universe. Identical to the input of run_enrichment.

n_perm

Integer. Initial permutations per pathway. Default 1000L. For borderline pathways under adaptive_mode = "refine", additional permutations are drawn until the Wilson CI resolves.

adaptive

Logical. Use adaptive permutation stopping? Default TRUE. Strongly recommended for batch runs: saves substantial computation on clear results.

adaptive_mode

Character. One of "refine" (default), "pvalue", or "decision". See run_enrichment for details. "refine" is best for batch use.

alpha

Numeric. Significance level for BH adjustment and adaptive stopping decisions. Default 0.05.

eps

Numeric. RSE threshold for adaptive_mode = "pvalue". Default 0.01.

min_size

Integer. Pathways with fewer than min_size genes (after intersection with names(gene_stats)) are silently skipped. Default 10L.

max_size

Integer. Pathways with more than max_size genes are silently skipped. Default 500L.

nthreads

Positive integer. Parallel workers via parallel::mclapply. Default 1L. Silently reduced to 1 on Windows.

seed

Optional integer. Sets the global RNG seed and derives per-pathway seeds to ensure reproducible results with any nthreads value.

...

Additional arguments passed to run_enrichment (e.g. gsea_weight, alternative).

Value

A list with two named elements:

results

Named list of run_enrichment outputs, one per pathway that passed size filtering and ran successfully. Directly compatible with run_gsea_redundancy_selection.

summary

Data frame (sorted by padj) with one row per pathway. Columns: pathway, set_size, enrichment_score, p_value, padj (BH), overlap, leading_edge_size. When adaptive = TRUE: also perms_used and converged.

Details

This function replaces the boilerplate lapply loop that users would otherwise write to produce input for run_gsea_redundancy_selection. It adds size filtering, optional parallelism, progress reporting, per-pathway error recovery, and BH adjustment.

Adaptive permutations for batch runs

With adaptive = TRUE and adaptive_mode = "refine" (default), each pathway first runs n_perm fixed permutations. Only pathways whose p-value lands in the borderline zone near alpha receive additional permutations. In a typical Reactome analysis most pathways are either clearly non-significant (stop early) or very significant (also stop early) – only a minority require the full refinement budget. Across 1 500 pathways this can halve the total permutation count compared to fixed n_perm for every pathway.

Parallelism

Uses parallel::mclapply (fork-based, Linux/macOS). Each worker receives a deterministic seed derived from seed + pathway_index, so results are reproducible regardless of nthreads. Silently falls back to sequential on Windows where forking is unavailable. Memory cost per worker is negligible because gene_stats (a named numeric vector) is small relative to the expression matrix used in run_info_assoc.

See also

run_enrichment for single-pathway GSEA, run_gsea_redundancy_selection for the downstream redundancy selection step.

Examples

set.seed(1)
universe   <- paste0("G", 1:1000)
gene_stats <- setNames(rnorm(1000), universe)
gene_stats[universe[1:50]] <- gene_stats[universe[1:50]] + 3

gene_sets <- list(
  pathway_A  = universe[1:50],
  pathway_A2 = c(universe[1:45], universe[51:55]),
  pathway_B  = universe[500:540],
  pathway_N  = sample(universe[200:400], 40)
)

batch <- run_batch_gsea(
  gene_sets  = gene_sets,
  gene_stats = gene_stats,
  n_perm     = 500L,
  adaptive   = TRUE,
  seed       = 1L
)
#> Running GSEA on 4 pathways [adaptive = TRUE, mode = 'refine', nthreads = 1].
#> Done. 2 / 4 pathways significant at padj <= 0.05.
#> Refine mode: 4/4 pathways resolved at 500 perms; 0 borderline pathways refined (0 extra perms).

print(batch$summary)
#>      pathway set_size enrichment_score     p_value overlap leading_edge_size
#> 1  pathway_A       50        0.9470285 0.001996008      50                46
#> 2 pathway_A2       50        0.9116016 0.001996008      50                41
#> 3  pathway_B       41       -0.2444636 0.862275449      41                12
#> 4  pathway_N       40       -0.2616645 0.870259481      40                14
#>   perms_used converged        padj
#> 1        500      TRUE 0.003992016
#> 2        500      TRUE 0.003992016
#> 3        500      TRUE 0.870259481
#> 4        500      TRUE 0.870259481

# Pipe directly into redundancy selection
sel <- run_gsea_redundancy_selection(
  gsea_results = batch$results,
  gene_stats   = gene_stats,
  alpha        = 0.05,
  seed         = 1L
)
#> 2 / 4 pathways significant at alpha = 0.05.
#> Leading-edge union: 46 genes (4.6% of universe).
#> Computing IT enrichment scores on leading edges...
#> Running CMI redundancy selection on leading edges...
#> Auto-selected min_gain = 0.04048 bits (noise floor = 0.0032, relevance threshold = 0.04048 [15% of leading MI]).
#> Stopping at step 2: best conditional gain = 0 bits < min_gain = 0.04048.
print(sel)
#>           step   pathway set_size marginal_mi conditional_mi redundancy_ratio
#> pathway_A    1 pathway_A       46   0.2698772      0.2698772                1
#>           cumulative_bits     p_value enrichment_score leading_edge_size
#> pathway_A       0.2698772 0.001996008        0.9470285                46
#>           full_set_size
#> pathway_A            50
